skill.name: entra-agent-id
skill.description: Provision Microsoft Entra Agent Identity Blueprints, BlueprintPrincipals, and per-instance Agent Identities via Microsoft Graph, and configure OAuth 2.0 token exchange (fmi_path, OBO, cross-tenant) including the Microsoft Entra SDK for AgentID sidecar. USE FOR: Agent Identity Blueprint, BlueprintPrincipal, agent OAuth, fmi_path token exchange, agent OBO, Workload Identity Federation for agents, polyglot agent auth, Microsoft.Identity.Web.AgentIdentities. DO NOT USE FOR: standard Entra app registration (use entra-app-registration), Azure RBAC (use azure-rbac), Microsoft Foundry agent authoring (use microsoft-foundry).
skill.summary: Provision Microsoft Entra Agent Identity Blueprints and per-instance Agent Identities via Microsoft Graph with OAuth 2.0 token exchange support
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate Microsoft-authored administrative skill for provisioning Entra Agent Identities. Static findings are primarily documentation patterns (CLI command syntax in backtick notation) and standard Azure credential environment variable usage. All network traffic targets official Microsoft Graph endpoints. No command injection vulnerabilities detected - subprocess calls use hardcoded argument strings. The skill is safe to publish with appropriate documentation.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for Azure Credentials
low_findings.description: Skill accesses standard Azure credential environment variables (AZURE_TENANT_ID, AZURE_CLIENT_ID, AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET) for application-based authentication to Microsoft Graph. This is standard Entra management practice.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment variable access is for legitimate Azure authentication patterns documented by Microsoft.

content.user_title: Configure Microsoft Entra Agent Identities for AI Agents
content.value_statement: Managing agent identities in Microsoft Entra requires navigating complex API constraints, OAuth 2.0 flows, and federated identity configurations. This skill provides step-by-step guidance for provisioning Agent Identity Blueprints, per-instance identities, and runtime token exchange for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code agents.
actual_capabilities: Create Agent Identity Blueprints and BlueprintPrincipals via Microsoft Graph API
Provision per-instance Agent Identities with proper sponsorship structure
Configure OAuth 2.0 token exchange flows including fmi_path, OBO, and cross-tenant scenarios
Set up Federated Identity Credentials for Workload Identity Federation on Blueprints
Deploy the Microsoft Entra SDK for AgentID sidecar for polyglot agent authentication (Python, Node, Go, Java)
Grant application (appRoleAssignments) and delegated (oauth2PermissionGrants) permissions to Agent Identities
limitations: Sponsors must be Users at Blueprint creation time (Groups supported for Agent Identity sponsors only)
DefaultAzureCredential is not supported due to Directory.AccessAsUser.All rejection; use ClientSecretCredential or Connect-MgGraph with explicit scopes
Permission propagation delay of 30-120+ seconds after admin consent requires exponential backoff retry logic
Sequential API requests (Blueprint then Principal then Identity) may fail without delays due to Entra propagation timing

use_cases.title: Enterprise AI Agent Deployment
use_cases.description: Provision secure, auditable identities for AI agents deployed in Azure. Create a Blueprint for your agent type, generate per-instance Agent Identities for each deployment, and configure federated identity credentials for production workloads.
use_cases.target_user: Cloud architects and DevOps teams deploying AI agents at scale

use_cases.title: Multi-Agent Orchestration
use_cases.description: Manage complex multi-agent systems where each agent needs distinct permissions and audit trails. Configure cross-tenant token flows for agents that need to access resources across different Entra tenants.
use_cases.target_user: Solutions architects building agentic AI systems

use_cases.title: Development and Testing Environments
use_cases.description: Set up local dev environments with client secrets for testing Agent Identity flows before moving to production Managed Identity configurations. Use the sidecar container for polyglot agent development.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers integrating agents with Microsoft Entra

prompt_templates.title: Create Agent Identity Blueprint
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a new Agent Identity Blueprint in Microsoft Entra for my [agent-type] agent. Use PowerShell with delegated scopes: AgentIdentityBlueprint.Create, AgentIdentityBlueprint.ReadWrite.All, AgentIdentityBlueprintPrincipal.Create, AgentIdentity.Create.All, User.Read.
prompt_templates.scenario: Initial setup of agent identity infrastructure

prompt_templates.title: Configure Runtime Token Exchange
prompt_templates.prompt: Walk me through setting up the fmi_path runtime token exchange for an autonomous agent. I have a Blueprint with a Federated Identity Credential configured for a system-assigned Managed Identity.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy AgentID Sidecar
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me deploy the Microsoft Entra SDK for AgentID sidecar container for my Python-based agent. I need to configure it for autonomous authentication (no user context).

prompt_templates.title: Debug Agent ID Error
prompt_templates.prompt: I am receiving error [AADSTS82001 or AADSTS700211 or PropertyNotCompatibleWithAgentIdentity]. Help me diagnose and resolve this Agent ID provisioning issue.

output_examples.input: Create an Agent Identity Blueprint named 'data-processing-agent'
output_examples.output: Successfully created Blueprint 'data-processing-agent' with ID 'abc123'. Next step: Create the BlueprintPrincipal using POST /servicePrincipals/microsoft.graph.agentIdentityBlueprintPrincipal. Note: You must use a User as sponsor at Blueprint creation (Groups not supported).

output_examples.input: Set up fmi_path token exchange with Managed Identity
output_examples.prompt: Configure federated identity credential for production agent
output_examples.output: FIC created on Blueprint: issuer='https://login.microsoftonline.com/tenant-id/v2.0', subject='mi-principal-id', audiences=['api://AzureADTokenExchange']. Runtime exchange flow: Agent Identity requests token from Managed Identity, then exchanges via fmi_path for downstream API access.

best_practices: Use delegated permissions instead of application permissions where possible to avoid Directory.AccessAsUser.All rejection issues
Add exponential backoff retry logic (30-120 second delays) after granting permissions to account for Entra propagation delays
Store Blueprint App ID as configuration; Agent Identities inherit Blueprint credentials and should not hold their own secrets
anti_patterns: Do not use DefaultAzureCredential for Agent Identity authentication - Azure CLI tokens carry Directory.AccessAsUser.All which causes 403 errors
Do not create Agent Identities without first creating a BlueprintPrincipal - BlueprintPrincipal is not auto-created and is required for identity creation
Do not grant app permissions directly to BlueprintPrincipals - grant permissions to individual Agent Identities instead

faq.question: Why is my Agent Identity returning 403 Forbidden?
faq.answer: The most common cause is Directory.AccessAsUser.All on your token - this permission causes all other Agent ID permissions to be ignored. Switch to application permissions (ClientSecretCredential) or use Connect-MgGraph with explicit delegated scopes excluding Directory.AccessAsUser.All.

faq.question: How do I create Blueprints for production vs. development?
faq.answer: Production Blueprints should use Federated Identity Credentials with Managed Identity. Development environments can use client secrets for simpler testing. Never hardcode secrets in code - use environment variables or Azure Key Vault.

faq.question: Can I use Groups as Blueprint sponsors?
faq.answer: No. Blueprint sponsors must be Users at creation time. However, Agent Identity sponsors can be either Users or Groups, providing more flexibility after the Blueprint is created.

faq.question: What is the difference between Blueprint and BlueprintPrincipal?
faq.answer: Blueprint is the application object that holds credentials and defines the agent type. BlueprintPrincipal is the service principal for the Blueprint in your tenant - it is NOT auto-created and must be explicitly created before Agent Identities can be provisioned.

faq.question: How do polyglot agents authenticate without Microsoft.Identity.Web?
faq.answer: Use the Microsoft Entra SDK for AgentID sidecar container. Deploy the sidecar alongside your agent, configure SIDECAR_URL and AGENT_CLIENT_ID environment variables, and call the sidecar endpoints for token exchange instead of using the .NET library directly.

faq.question: Why am I getting 'PropertyNotCompatibleWithAgentIdentity' errors?
faq.answer: This error occurs when trying to add credentials directly to an Agent Identity. Agent Identities cannot hold credentials - all credentials (passwords, certificates, FICs) must be configured on the Blueprint only. Agent Identities inherit credentials from their Blueprint at runtime.
